questions.q: What is the primary function of the CPU?
questions.explanation: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is responsible for executing instructions and performing calculations. It fetches, decodes, and executes instructions, making it the primary function of the CPU.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of RAM?
questions.explanation: Dynamic RAM (DRAM) is a type of Random Access Memory (RAM) that stores data in capacitors, making it volatile.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a cache memory?
questions.explanation: Cache memory is a small, fast memory that stores frequently accessed data, reducing the access time and improving system performance.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of computer bus?
questions.explanation: ISA (Industry Standard Architecture) bus, PCI (Peripheral Component Interconnect) bus, and IDE (Integrated Drive Electronics) bus are all types of computer buses that provide communication between components.

questions.q: What is the difference between a 32-bit and 64-bit operating system?
opts: (C) Both the word size and memory address size
questions.explanation: A 64-bit operating system has both a 64-bit word size and a 64-bit memory address size, allowing it to access more memory and perform calculations more efficiently.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of storage device?
questions.explanation: A Hard Disk Drive (HDD) is a type of storage device that stores data on a magnetic disk.

questions.q: What is the primary function of a network interface card?
opts: (A) To connect the CPU to the keyboard
(B) To connect the CPU to the monitor
(C) To connect the computer to a network
(D) To connect the computer to a printer
questions.explanation: A Network Interface Card (NIC) is responsible for connecting a computer to a network, allowing it to communicate with other devices.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of network protocol?
questions.explanation: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol), HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol), and FTP (File Transfer Protocol) are all types of network protocols used for communication between devices.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a power supply unit?
opts: (C) To convert AC power to DC power
questions.explanation: A Power Supply Unit (PSU) is responsible for converting Alternating Current (AC) power from the mains to Direct Current (DC) power for the computer's components.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of input device?
questions.explanation: A Monitor, Keyboard, and Mouse are all types of input devices that allow users to interact with the computer.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a hard drive?
questions.explanation: A hard drive is a type of storage device that stores data permanently on a magnetic disk.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of computer system?
questions.explanation: A Desktop computer, Laptop computer, and Server computer are all types of computer systems used for different purposes.

questions.q: What is the difference between a compiler and an interpreter?
opts: (C) Both the compilation time and execution time
questions.explanation: A compiler translates the entire source code into machine code at once, while an interpreter translates and executes the source code line by line, making compilation time and execution time different.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of computer virus?
questions.explanation: A Trojan horse, Worm, and Rootkit are all types of computer viruses that can harm a computer system.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a firewall?
opts: (C) To filter incoming traffic based on rules
questions.explanation: A firewall is a network security system that filters incoming traffic based on rules, allowing or blocking specific types of traffic.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of operating system?
questions.explanation: A Single-user operating system, Multi-user operating system, and Single-task operating system are all types of operating systems used for different purposes.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a database management system?
opts: (C) To manage data storage and retrieval
questions.explanation: A Database Management System (DBMS) is responsible for managing data storage and retrieval in a database.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of computer network?
questions.explanation: A LAN (Local Area Network), WAN (Wide Area Network), and MAN (Metropolitan Area Network) are all types of computer networks used for different purposes.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a load balancer?
opts: (A) To distribute incoming traffic across multiple servers
(B) To block incoming traffic to a single server
questions.explanation: A Load Balancer is a network device that distributes incoming traffic across multiple servers, improving system performance and availability.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of computer security threat?
questions.explanation: Malware, Phishing, and SQL injection are all types of computer security threats that can harm a computer system.
